C++ surface integral equation (SIE) code for homogeneous and layered media scattering

 Debug helper: read a value of type T and print it to std::cout.
 

Detailed Description

Orchestration, file I/O, and batch runs.

Function Documentation

◆ ReadAndPrint()

template<class T>
int ReadAndPrint ( std::ifstream * inStream)

Debug helper: read a value of type T and print it to std::cout.

Uses ReadCommented to attempt a read from inStream; prints "Error!" if no value could be read/parsed, otherwise prints the value followed by a newline. The function always returns 0 (it is intended for quick debugging).

Template Parameters
TDestination type (must be stream-extractable via operator>>).
Parameters
[in,out]inStreamPointer to an open std::ifstream to read from.
Returns
Always 0.

Definition at line 118 of file iofunctions.h.

Here is the call graph for this function:

◆ ReadCommented()

template<class T>
int ReadCommented ( std::ifstream * inStream,
T * target )

Read a value of type T from a text stream, skipping lines beginning with '#'.

Attempts to extract the next non-comment token from inStream and parse it into target using operator>> on a temporary std::stringstream. If the next token starts with '#', the remainder of that line is discarded and the search continues. The function returns immediately after parsing a single value or if end-of-file/error is reached.

Template Parameters
TDestination type (must be stream-extractable via operator>>).
Parameters
[in,out]inStreamPointer to an open std::ifstream to read from.
[out]targetPointer to the destination variable to receive the value.
Returns
0 on success; 1 on failure to parse or on reaching EOF without reading a value.
Behavior
  • Skips tokens that begin with '#', consuming the rest of that line.
  • Accepts multiple values per line; stops after extracting the first valid value.
  • Leaves the stream positioned after the consumed token (or end of file on failure).
Warning
This function assumes that comment markers appear as the first character of a token. Comments that appear after a value on the same line must be separated by whitespace (e.g., "42 # note").

Definition at line 54 of file iofunctions.h.

Here is the caller graph for this function:

◆ ReadCommentedG()

template<class T>
int ReadCommentedG ( std::ifstream * inStream,
T * target )

Read a value of type T from a text stream, skipping lines beginning with '$'.

Identical semantics to ReadCommented except that lines whose first token starts with '$' are treated as comments.

Template Parameters
TDestination type (must be stream-extractable via operator>>).
Parameters
[in,out]inStreamPointer to an open std::ifstream to read from.
[out]targetPointer to the destination variable to receive the value.
Returns
0 on success; 1 on failure to parse or on reaching EOF without reading a value.

Definition at line 86 of file iofunctions.h.
